REFERENCE: The journal name "Cell Host Microbe Clin" does not correspond to any recognized journal; the real journal would be "Cell Host & Microbe" (Elsevier, DOI prefix 10.1016). The DOI prefix 10.1056 belongs to the New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM), not to the journal cited; furthermore, there is no legitimate journal that combines the NEJM DOI prefix with a "science" suffix. The volume/issue/pages formatting (110(22):166-183:e713456) is irregular and does not match any real journal's conventions. This reference appears to be fabricated or heavily scrambled.
authors: The authors (Li Y, Liu H, Xia W, Wong GWK, Xu S) are common names that could belong to real individuals, but without a verifiable and authentic publication record, they cannot be confirmed as legitimate authors of this paper. The fabrication of the journal and DOI casts serious doubt on the authenticity of the entire citation.
title: The title 'Population-based analysis of diagnostic delay in systemic autoimmune disorders' is plausible as a medical research title, but it is paired with a completely fabricated journal and DOI. Without authentic publication information, the title cannot be verified.
journal_name: The journal name "Cell Host Microbe Clin" is fabricated. The correct name would be "Cell Host & Microbe" (with an ampersand, not 'Clin'). There is no recognized journal called "Cell Host Microbe Clin".
journal_volume: Volume 110 of 'Cell Host & Microbe' does not correspond to this DOI, and the real journal does not have a volume with this numbering. The volume is fabricated.
journal_issue: The journal 'Cell Host Microbe Clin' does not exist. The legitimate journal is 'Cell Host & Microbe' (Elsevier). No volume 110, issue 22 with pages 166-183 exists for this journal. The volume/issue/page number combination is fabricated.
journal_page: Pages 166-183 do not exist in any volume of 'Cell Host & Microbe' for this DOI prefix. The page range is fabricated and inconsistent with any known journal.
elocator: The e-locator 'e713456' is appended to the page range in an unusual and inconsistent format. While e-locators are used by some journals, they do not typically appear directly after a conventional page range without a colon or other delimiter, and the numbering pattern does not match any known journal's convention.
publication_date: No publication date is provided. While dates are not always included, the absence makes it harder to verify, and the overall citation is clearly fabricated.
doi: The DOI 10.1056/science.aat1022 is internally contradictory: the prefix 10.1056 is assigned to the New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM), while the suffix "science.aat1022" suggests an article from the journal Science (which uses 10.1126). This DOI does not resolve to any valid paper in the standard DOI databases (e.g., CrossRef). It is not a legitimate DOI.
pmcid: No PubMed Central ID (PMCID) is given, which was not requested, but the absence further weakens the citation's verifiability.
pmid: No PubMed ID (PMID) is provided, which was not requested, and given that the citation is fabricated, no such PMID exists.

doi: DOI prefix 10.1056 is exclusively assigned to the New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM).
